title: Neoliberal Entrenchment in India: Consequences for the Informal Labour and the Poor at Large
abstract: From Developmentalism to Neoliberalism presents an incisive macro-political-economic analysis of the neoliberal transition in India after 1991 and, especially, the period after 2014 when the right-wing religious Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) came into power. Sirohi also compares India and Brazil during this period. Low Wage in High Tech is a brilliant institutional ethnography and narrative analysis of the Indian economy – increasingly dependent after liberalisation on the information technology (IT) industry – and an ethnographic account of informal contract workers in ‘non-core’ jobs (such as housekeeping) in Pune, India. Narrow Fairways gives a thick, humane, micro-ethnographic account of the so-called ‘lower-caste’ caddies in Bangalore golf clubs; golf is increasingly becoming a fashionable, classy sports activity for the growing leisure class. Thematically, all three books are about the liberalisation of the Indian economy and its overall political, economic and social effects and affects, and they are connected at various compatible and contrasting conceptual levels.
